title: campanula grandis subsp. grandis
intro: Campanula grandis subsp. grandis, also known as the Canterbury bell, is a plant species in the bellflower family (Campanulaceae) characterized by its large, bell-shaped flowers. Traditionally, the plant has been used in herbal medicine, and it is known to possess medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Campanula grandis subsp. grandis include its potential to reduce fever, alleviate respiratory issues, and support the immune system. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in teas, infusions, and tinctures to harness its medicinal properties and provide relief from various health conditions.
